title: Add CLI commands for new SDK features (tags, quick profiles, proxy validate, local/cloud)
description:
Add CLI coverage for recently added SDK features:
1. mlx tag (create, update, remove, assign, search)
2. mlx launcher quick-start / quick-save
3. mlx launcher validate-proxy
4. mlx profile create-local / create-cloud
Follow existing CLI patterns: flag.FlagSet, withRuntime, emit. Update help text and root dispatch. Add unit tests for new commands.

title: Add local/cloud profile creation helpers
description: Add Workflows.CreateLocalProfile and CreateCloudProfile convenience helpers that set Parameters.Storage.IsLocal and delegate to CreateProfilesAndVerify. Include mocked integration tests.

title: Add quick profiles and proxy validate to Launcher
description: Add Launcher.StartQuick, SaveQuick, ConvertQuickToProfile, ValidateProxy. Add typed models and mocked tests. Proxy validate uses POST /api/v1/proxy/validate with type/host/port/username/password payload.

title: Add Tags service to SDK
description: Add TagsService with Create, Update, Remove, AssignToProfiles, Search. Follow existing SDK patterns: typed request/response, bearer auth, status envelope, co-located tests. Wire into Client.

title: Fix stop wait semantics and extension enable verification
description: Address the follow-up review findings: make CLI stop wait require an explicit stopped status, and make the direct extension enable path verify that the profile binding actually appears instead of returning success after a single read.

title: Fix CLI config precedence and template/proxy default bugs
description: Address the review findings in the reference CLI: make retry.enabled=false work, stop command flags from bypassing config defaults, preserve template storage.is_local unless explicitly overridden, and apply configured proxy geography defaults. Add regression tests for each behavior change.

title: Finalize pending SDK and CLI worktree changes
description: Recover and finish the in-progress SDK/CLI change set left in the working tree. Validate the scope, complete any missing fixes, run quality gates, and prepare the branch for commit by breaking the work into coherent verified pieces.

title: Spike create 50 profiles cadence
description: Measure whether the SDK can create 50 profiles as 5 back-to-back create requests of 10 profiles each, determine whether any inter-request delay is required in practice, and capture the findings in an opt-in live spike with permanent cleanup.

title: Spike live profile creation limits and batch size
description: Investigate live Multilogin X profile creation limits for the current account and REST API. Verify rate-limit behavior around the user's stated 50 RPM, measure how many profiles can be created in one request/batch, and capture the findings in reproducible opt-in tests/docs. Cleanup must use permanent delete so trash-bin items do not consume profile quota.

title: Fix retry defaults and non-idempotent retry behavior
description: SDK retry handling currently retries even when WithRetry is not configured, and it can replay non-idempotent POST operations. Fix the transport behavior, add tests that reproduce both cases, and update README/docs to match the implementation.

title: Add retry and error classification
description: Strengthen the SDK for production reuse by adding retry/backoff helpers and typed error classification for MLX API and launcher calls. Cover transient network failures, rate limits, and retryable MLX responses so external CLI/worker projects can build on a resilient core instead of reimplementing transport heuristics.
close_reason: Implemented retry/backoff helpers, typed MLX error classification, tests, and docs.

title: Add MLX proxy workflows
description: Implement SDK support for Multilogin X proxy workflows that are critical for real automation projects. Cover profile-bound proxies with typed request models, prioritize SOCKS5 because HTTP proxy mode is known to behave incorrectly in live usage, and support affinity-oriented location fields such as country, region, and city so callers can keep a stable browser fingerprint and stable geography for a given account/profile over repeated sessions. Document the known HTTP proxy bug and any observed proxy lifetime/retention behavior from live validation.

title: Verify local profile semantics
description: Investigate Multilogin X local profile semantics across create and import flows. Compare API metadata from profile search and metas endpoints against observed UI storage state, add repeatable validation coverage, and document any live mismatches or limitations so SDK workflows do not rely on misleading signals.
notes: Refinement: remove fallback to raw ProfileMeta.IsLocal from SDK decision helpers. Treat profile/metas top-level is_local as diagnostic-only and rely on parameters.storage.is_local plus search/launcher-confirmed semantics.

title: Add extension object workflows
description: Implement SDK support and documentation for extension object workflows validated by live research. Capture the known quirk that adding a local zip extension to a local profile requires using a cloud object, and investigate/document behavior when adding extensions by Chrome Web Store ID for more realistic user-like setups.

title: Add templates/resources service
description: Implement SDK support for Multilogin X template/resource object workflows validated by live API research: list resource types, list template metas, fetch resource meta by ID, inspect profile usages, create profile templates via object_storage/create_and_upload, download template bodies, and manage safe delete/restore flows. Capture the live behavior that profile templates are resource objects and that the full template payload lives in object_body while resources/:id/meta only returns object metadata.

title: Investigate live template endpoints
description: Probe the live Multilogin X template-related resource/object-storage endpoints with the real MLX token, identify how existing templates are listed and fetched, verify create/update semantics without deleting the existing template named Template, and document any live API quirks.

title: Run live launcher health E2E and commit
description: Add or validate a live E2E check for launcher readiness using the version-backed health probe, run the gated E2E test against a real Multilogin X desktop instance, and commit the SDK changes if the live version response succeeds.

title: Add launcher health check endpoint
description: Research whether the Multilogin X desktop launcher exposes a dedicated health or liveness endpoint, and add an SDK method for startup availability checks. If a dedicated health endpoint does not exist, document and implement the safest launcher readiness probe available.

title: Validate cookie flow against live MLX API
description: Run live integration/end-to-end validation for the cookie flow against the real Multilogin X APIs using the configured MLX_TOKEN, capture any request/response mismatches, and fix the SDK accordingly.
close_reason: Live MLX validation completed; profile lifecycle, transfer/import-export, and cookie seeding flows now pass reliably against the real API.

title: Add cookie seeding E2E coverage
description: Add gated end-to-end coverage for pre-made cookie website discovery, cookie metadata creation/update, cookie seeding helper execution, and launcher cookie export validation against the real Multilogin X APIs.

title: Add cookie seeding helper
description: Implement a high-level helper that creates or updates pre-made cookie metadata, fetches generated cookie bundles, resolves the profile folder when needed, and imports cookies into the profile with typed results and tests.

title: Add cookies service to SDK
description: Implement first-class cookie operations in the Go SDK, including pre-made cookies target website listing, cookies metadata create/update, cookies list retrieval, and launcher cookie import/export support with typed models and tests.

title: Add CLI commands for new SDK features
description:
The SDK now supports Tags, Quick Profiles, Proxy Validate, and Local/Cloud profile helpers. The reference CLI (cmd/mlx) should expose commands for these features:

- mlx tag (create, update, remove, assign, search)
- mlx launcher quick-start / quick-save
- mlx launcher validate-proxy
- mlx profile create-local / create-cloud

This depends on the current SDK surface being stable.

title: Audit local/service directories before GitHub publish
description: Review repository directories and tracked service artifacts to identify local-only, generated, or agent-specific paths that should be ignored before publishing the repository to GitHub.
notes: Audited root/service directories for GitHub publishing. Confirmed local-only directories .claude, .firecrawl, .tmp, and docs/superpowers are ignored. Removed already-tracked .beads directory and tracked docs/superpowers file from the Git index while keeping local files.

title: Polish SDK codebase for Go style, cleanup, and architecture audit
description: Audit the repository against official Go style guidance, normalize readable formatting and internal naming where appropriate, identify duplicates/clutter/dead code, review test layout and architecture tradeoffs, and implement low-risk cleanup improvements with verification.
notes: Completed cleanup pass: documented Go style baseline, added .editorconfig, normalized tracked Go files with gofmt, decomposed internal/cli/root.go into themed files, and verified with go test ./... plus go vet ./.... Remaining notes captured in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-18-sdk-cleanup-audit.md.

title: Run live Rod-to-Playwright E2E validation
description: Validate that AutomationRod now normalizes through Playwright/CDP end-to-end by creating a real profile, starting it, resolving the websocket URL, and attaching with Rod through the SDK-managed path.

title: Ignore local agent and workspace folders
description: Update repository ignore rules so local agent/workspace directories such as .beads, .claude, .firecrawl, and .tmp are not committed when publishing the repository upstream. Note: .git itself is internal to Git and does not need a .gitignore rule.

title: Implement launcher normalization and endpoint helpers
description: Implement Task 2 in the root SDK package: add focused launcher automation normalization and endpoint resolution helpers, introduce a typed AutomationEndpointError, extend StartedProfileData with requested/launcher automation metadata plus CDP port support, and make Launcher.Start normalize rod to playwright while preserving returned metadata. Scope is limited to launcher.go, automation_endpoints.go, launcher_test.go, and automation_endpoints_test.go.

title: Redesign launcher automation and CDP helpers
description: Design and implement the Rod-to-Playwright launcher alias, CDP/WebSocket helpers, and higher-level automation workflow ergonomics for the SDK. The work must be test-driven, include code-quality cleanup in touched areas, and update docs to reflect the new automation contract.

title: Find GitHub-related skills for publishing repo to GitHub
description: Identify Codex/agent skills that help work with GitHub and the GitHub API so the repository can be published upstream later.

title: Investigate Superpowers and install relevant Codex CLI skills locally
description: The user asked to identify what 'Superpowers' refers to using the Firecrawl skill and install the relevant Codex CLI skills locally in this repository.

title: Re-review CLI after config precedence fixes
description: Perform a follow-up review of the current HEAD after the CLI config precedence fixes to catch any remaining behavioral inconsistencies or missing verification gaps.

title: Review recent SDK/CLI cleanup changes
description: Perform a code review pass over the recent verified-workflows, reference CLI, and cleanup commits. Identify bugs, behavioral regressions, API/UX inconsistencies, and missing test coverage with concrete file references.

title: Tidy docs and CLI follow-up cleanup
description: Run a cleanup pass across docs, reference CLI, and adjacent packages to remove leftover artifacts, tighten inconsistencies, and fix obvious quality issues that remain after the large SDK/CLI landing.

title: Create one local MLX profile
description: Create exactly one local Multilogin X profile through the current SDK/CLI setup for live validation. No code changes are required unless blocked by missing runtime configuration.
close_reason: Live profile creation was attempted, but MultiloginX returned 501 for POST /profile/create and POST /profile/clone. User stopped further attempts after confirming this is an MLX-side issue.

title: Add CLI profile creation from template
description: Add reference CLI support for creating a profile from an existing profile template resource. Support selecting a template by id and creating a local or cloud profile with optional overrides such as name and proxy assignment. Update docs and examples accordingly.

title: Add CLI template list/get commands
description: Add reference CLI support for listing and inspecting profile template resources so operators can discover available templates and inspect one template before using it for profile creation. Scope includes command help, output integration, and docs updates.

title: Reorganize test layout by scope
description: Restructure tests into clearer unit, integration, and e2e organization without losing coverage or breaking go test flows. Separate doc/example tests cleanly where useful, keep root module test execution simple, and update docs or package layout as needed so the repository looks polished and easier to navigate.

title: Design richer CLI config model
description: Add a more capable CLI configuration model for the planned reference CLI. Define config file shape, environment variable precedence, profile/folder defaults, output formatting options, retry/timeout settings, and launcher/base URL overrides. Preserve the rule that MLX_TOKEN is the only auth input and no interactive auth is needed. Document the config behavior clearly for consumers.

title: Add reference CLI scaffold
description: Build a consumer-facing CLI under cmd/ for mlx-go-sdk with a polished user experience and documentation. Scope: folder/profile/launcher/export/import/extension/cookies/proxy oriented commands only; do NOT include mobile profile commands. Authentication must rely only on MLX_TOKEN and existing SDK environment configuration; do NOT add interactive login or auth flows. Include a command layout, shared client bootstrap, helpful output/error handling, and docs showing how the CLI maps onto SDK workflows.

title: Add verified high-level workflows
description: Expand the high-level workflow layer with verified post-condition checks so operations are not considered successful only because a request returned without error. Build reusable flows for create/find/start/stop/import/export/extension attachment using confirmed signals and explicit verification.

title: Add production examples and consumer docs
description: Add consumer-oriented examples and documentation for using the SDK as a reusable module in external projects. Cover realistic flows such as profile creation, Rod attachment, local profile handling, extensions, cookies, imports/exports, and safe production patterns so teams can structure CLI tools under cmd/ with confidence.

title: Add archive edge-case coverage
description: Expand archive manager tests for edge cases such as missing files, extension normalization, overwrite handling, and partial export/import failures.

title: Add polling retry and backoff helpers
description: Add reusable retry/backoff utilities for launcher status checks and transfer import/export polling to improve resilience against transient launcher delays.

title: Expand typed response models
description: Broaden typed model coverage for profile, launcher, and transfer payloads so SDK users need less manual map or raw JSON handling.

title: Add profile lookup helpers
description: Provide convenience helpers to resolve profiles by name or other common lookup keys without requiring consumers to handcraft search requests and filtering logic.

title: Bootstrap local git repository
description: Initialize the local repository baseline by reviewing ignore rules, staging the intended project files, verifying tests pass, and creating the first local commit without configuring any remote.

title: Research pre-made cookies endpoints
description: Inspect the Multilogin X Postman collection for pre-made cookies, cookie import/export, and cookie metadata endpoints that can be used to seed believable browsing history for profiles, then summarize what the SDK is missing.

title: Add filesystem export manager layer
description: Implement a filesystem-oriented export manager layer that organizes exported profile archives into per-profile folders without ever renaming the zip file itself, plus unit tests and documentation for safe archive handling.

title: Add import-export E2E coverage
description: Add gated end-to-end coverage for profile export and import flows, verify export path semantics from the real MultiloginX API, and document important operational constraints around profile quota and permanent deletion versus trash.

title: Extend MultiloginX CRUD E2E flow
description: Expand the gated end-to-end profile lifecycle test to cover profile search, update, partial update, soft delete, restore, launcher start/status/stop, and final cleanup against the real MultiloginX API.

title: Add MultiloginX E2E vertical
description: Add a gated end-to-end test flow for folders, profiles, and launcher operations using MLX_TOKEN and explicit environment flags, starting with create/start/status/stop/delete behavior.

title: Strengthen MultiloginX typed models
description: Replace generic profile parameter maps with stronger typed request and response models for flags, storage, fingerprint, proxy, and related profile lifecycle payloads in the Go SDK.

title: Scaffold MultiloginX Go SDK architecture
description: Create the initial Go SDK layout inspired by strong patterns from go-github and godo, with service-oriented design, TDD workflow, and room for unit, integration, and E2E tests against the real API.

title: Select MultiloginX SDK MVP endpoints
description: Review the extracted MultiloginX Postman endpoint inventory and decide which profile, launcher, import/export, and folder endpoints belong in the first SDK version while excluding billing, account, and unrelated APIs.

title: Scaffold MultiloginX Go SDK architecture
description: Create the initial Go SDK layout inspired by strong patterns from go-github and godo, with service-oriented design, TDD workflow, and room for unit, integration, and E2E tests against the real API.

title: Research MultiloginX API surface
description: Investigate the Postman collection safely without dumping the full JSON, look for skills/workflows for large JSON handling, and identify profile-related endpoints that belong in the Go SDK while excluding billing/account management endpoints.

title: Add Rod integration examples
description: Add practical examples showing how to start profiles and connect Rod to the returned automation port for browser automation.

title: Add batch operation helpers
description: Provide helpers for performing common bulk operations across multiple profiles with clear error aggregation behavior.

title: Add high-level workflow helpers
description: Introduce high-level workflows that combine lower-level SDK calls for common tasks such as prepare-start-stop-export sequences.

title: Add roadmap issues for next SDK milestones
description: Track the next planned SDK milestones after the current MVP: profile lookup helper, broader typed model coverage, retry/backoff for launcher and transfer polling, high-level workflows, Rod examples, batch helpers, and archive edge-case tests.

value: CLI auth rule: use MLX_TOKEN and existing SDK environment configuration only; do not add interactive login/auth flows.

Proxy API live note: profile-proxy /v1/proxy/connection_url docs show data as a single string but live API may return an array of connection strings; sticky SOCKS5 requests with same country/region/city reused the account prefix and secret but changed sid between calls, so workflows should preserve parsed affinity/session metadata instead of assuming exact credential reuse.

Proxy follow-up created at 2026-04-20T17:59:48.4435630+04:00. Live SOCKS5 proxy was generated with country=us, region=new_jersey, city=east_brunswick. On the next return to this project, if about 24 hours or more have passed since this timestamp, verify whether requesting a new MLX proxy with the same geo filters still returns the same geography (country/region/city). Do not assume the same exact proxy credentials or sid; validate geo retention explicitly.
CLI scope: no mobile profile commands are needed for this repository's reference CLI.

Extension workflows: extensions are resource objects (type 6811b909-2e4b-45db-ab62-f14f515523cf); safest local-profile attach path is cloud-backed extension objects. Live note: create_extension_from_url with a Chrome Web Store-derived CRX URL currently fails in launcher with 500/404 fetch error, and creating a profile with storage.is_local=true may still return is_local=false in current environment.

Workflow rule: probe and E2E flows must validate post-conditions explicitly (status fields, follow-up reads, and cleanup results) instead of assuming success from request completion alone; UI/API mismatches must be called out as verification gaps.
CLI planning note: richer configuration is desired, including defaults and transport/output settings, as part of the reference CLI effort.

Extension quirk: for a local profile, adding an extension from a local zip requires using a cloud object reference. Adding extensions by Chrome Web Store ID may be useful for realistic user-like setups and needs live validation.

Profile templates are resource objects: list via /api/v1/resources/metas filtered by object_type_id=7e46e7f9-15d4-41b6-83b9-a652336793ec, create via launcher /api/v1/object_storage/create_and_upload, full template config lives in object_body, while resources/:id/meta returns only object metadata. Download via launcher /api/v1/object_storage/:id/download to materialize stored body.

Test scope note: this repo contains nested fixture/example projects under GithubExamples/. Avoid broad workspace-level test execution that picks up godo/metrics/TestTimeJSON. For SDK validation, run Go tests from the root module (for example: go test . or go test ./...) so only mlx-go-sdk packages are exercised.
